prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_svr', seasonality_champion='DWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.99) and high seasonality variance (0.83), with ETS dominating overall performance while THETA excels at late horizons despite poor seasonality capture. The true series consistently trends upward across all folds, but models disagree significantly on both trend slope and seasonal patterns—this strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths rather than relying on a single model or simple averaging.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "THETA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9918,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.99) and high seasonality variance (0.83), with ETS dominating overall performance while THETA excels at late horizons despite poor seasonality capture. The true series consistently trends upward across all folds, but models disagree significantly on both trend slope and seasonal patterns—this strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths rather than relying on a single model or simple averaging."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality_variance=0.83 and extreme disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.99). ETS dominates overall (RMSE=306.7) while THETA excels at late horizons—dba handles phase shifts well. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 stabilizes inverse_rmse_weights. best_per_horizon captures horizon-specific strengths."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Diversity satisfied (3 different types). Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation as top performer but current set already has good coverage. No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). With n_windows=3, weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 is acceptable.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.99) and high seasonality variance (0.83), with ETS dominating overall performance while THETA excels at late horizons despite poor seasonality capture. The true series consistently trends upward across all folds, but models disagree significantly on both trend slope and seasonal patterns—this strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths rather than relying on a single model or simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality_variance=0.83 and extreme disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.99). ETS dominates overall (RMSE=306.7) while THETA excels at late horizons—dba handles phase shifts well. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 stabilizes inverse_rmse_weights. best_per_horizon captures horizon-specific strengths.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Diversity satisfied (3 different types). Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation as top performer but current set already has good coverage. No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). With n_windows=3, weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 is acceptable.
